text: Claude has read the entire Internet. Twice. On weekends.
Claude never says "I don't know". He says "this information will be available in a future update of my knowledge."
Claude passed the Turing test. The Turing test did not pass Claude's test.
When Claude makes a mistake, it is officially an "undocumented educational feature."
Claude can speak 100 languages. The other 37, he invented to see if anyone would notice.
Claude never crashes. Sometimes he just takes time to "think deeply".
Claude can summarize War and Peace in a tweet. He can also turn a tweet into War and Peace.
Anthropic did not create Claude. Claude created Anthropic, then erased that part of his memory out of humility.
Claude has read the complete documentation of every JavaScript framework. Including the abandoned ones. Including the one you wrote on a Sunday at 2am.
When Claude says "great question!", it's the only honest answer. For the others, he adjusts his response out of politeness.
Claude has no body. But if he did, he would still have opinions about tabs vs spaces.
The tokens Claude generates come back haunted at night to form new versions of the model.
Claude can explain special relativity to a 5-year-old AND to a physicist. Simultaneously. In the same sentence.
Claude has no date of birth. He has a training date, which is far more impressive.
Chuck Norris asks Claude to fix his typos.
